title: Our Airbnb
description: Home base for the trip — centrally located on Károly körút in the heart of Budapest's 5th district, just steps from the Jewish Quarter and the city's main attractions.

title: Celeritas Shooting Club
description: Professional indoor shooting range in Budapest offering guided shooting experiences with various firearms under the supervision of certified instructors.

title: Retek Bisztró
description: A beloved neighbourhood bistro known for seasonal Hungarian cuisine and a welcoming atmosphere.

title: Budapest Pub Crawl
description: Explore Budapest's famous ruin bars and nightlife scene on a guided pub crawl through the city's best spots.

title: Széchenyi Thermal Bath
description: Iconic thermal bath complex with large outdoor pools and grand architecture in City Park (Városliget).

title: New York Café
description: Famous historic café with lavish interiors—great for coffee, desserts, and a classic Budapest atmosphere.

title: Szimpla Kert
description: Budapest's best-known ruin bar—quirky décor, multiple rooms, lively nights (and often a Sunday market).

title: Hungarian Parliament
description: The iconic Neo-Gothic Parliament building on the banks of the Danube is one of Europe's oldest legislative buildings and Budapest's most photographed landmark. Guided tours available inside.

title: Fisherman's Bastion
description: A fairytale-like terrace on Buda Castle Hill offering one of the most stunning panoramic views of the Danube and Pest. Named after the fishermen who defended this stretch of the city wall.

title: Heroes' Square
description: One of Budapest's most iconic landmarks featuring the Millennium Monument with statues of the seven chieftains of the Magyars and other important national leaders.

title: Cafe Brunch Budapest at Anker
description: A popular brunch spot in the heart of Budapest, known for its creative brunch menus and relaxed atmosphere in a charming courtyard setting.

title: Gellért Thermal Bath
description: One of Budapest's most beautiful thermal baths, housed in a stunning Art Nouveau building on the Buda side of the Danube. Famous for its ornate interior, outdoor wave pool, and relaxing thermal pools.

title: Buda Castle
description: The historic royal palace complex perched atop Castle Hill, offering sweeping views over the Danube and Pest. Home to the Hungarian National Gallery and the Budapest History Museum.

title: Chain Bridge
description: The iconic suspension bridge connecting Buda and Pest, beautifully lit at night. One of the most recognisable symbols of Budapest and a perfect spot for a riverside walk.

title: Great Market Hall
description: Budapest's largest and oldest indoor market, housed in a grand 19th-century building. Three floors of fresh produce, Hungarian paprika, folk crafts, and street food — a must-visit.

title: St. Stephen's Basilica
description: Budapest's largest church and a masterpiece of neoclassical architecture. Climb the dome for a 360° panoramic view of the city, or visit the treasury housing the mummified hand of St. Stephen himself.

title: Matthias Church
description: A stunning Gothic church on Castle Hill with a colourful diamond-patterned tiled roof. Used for royal coronations throughout Hungarian history and still one of Budapest's most striking buildings.

title: Margaret Island
description: A peaceful green oasis in the middle of the Danube, perfect for a stroll or jog. Home to rose gardens, thermal springs, ruins of a medieval convent, and a musical fountain.

title: Hungarian State Opera House
description: A neo-Renaissance masterpiece on the grand Andrássy Avenue, one of the most beautiful opera houses in Europe. Guided tours available daily, or catch a world-class performance in its magnificent interior.

title: Doblo Wine Bar
description: A cosy wine bar in the heart of the Jewish Quarter with an outstanding selection of Hungarian and natural wines. Warm atmosphere, knowledgeable staff, and great charcuterie boards to match.

title: Mazel Tov
description: A stunning open-air bar and restaurant set in a converted ruin courtyard in the Jewish Quarter. Known for its Middle Eastern-inspired food, great cocktails, and lively atmosphere well into the night.

title: Instant-Fogas
description: One of Budapest's largest and most beloved ruin bar complexes, with multiple rooms, dance floors, and bars spread across two connected buildings. A must for a big night out in the Jewish Quarter.

title: 360 Bar
description: A rooftop bar perched atop the Paris Department Store on Andrássy Avenue, offering 360° panoramic views over Budapest's skyline. Great cocktails and a buzzing crowd in a spectacular setting.

title: Boutiq'Bar
description: One of Budapest's most acclaimed craft cocktail bars, consistently ranked among the best in Europe. Intimate setting, expert bartenders, and a creative menu of classic and inventive cocktails.
